subsection: Early T-cell precursor ALL versus AML
title: Distinguishing Early T-cell Precursor ALL from AML with aberrant T-lineage antigen expression
learning_objectives: Differentiate ETP-ALL from AML with aberrant T-cell markers
Apply lineage-defining markers according to WHO5/ICC
clinical_vignette: A 27-year-old man presents with fever, fatigue and bruising. Bone marrow examination demonstrates an acute leukaemia. Flow cytometry shows a single blast population with stem-cell and myeloid-associated antigen expression.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Numerous blasts with fine chromatin and scant agranular cytoplasm. No Auer rods.
morphology.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow replaced by blasts without convincing granulocytic maturation.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: Strong cytoplasmic CD3 establishes T-lineage. Stem-cell/myeloid antigen co-expression with absent MPO favours ETP-ALL rather than AML.

molecular_genetics: NOTCH1 pending. FLT3 and NPM1 negative.
integrated_laboratory_diagnosis: Early T-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukaemia (ETP-ALL).
question: Which finding is most decisive in distinguishing this case from AML with aberrant T-cell antigen expression?
options: Strong cytoplasmic CD3 positivity with absent MPO
correct_answer: C. Strong cytoplasmic CD3 positivity with absent MPO
explanation: Cytoplasmic CD3 is the lineage-defining marker for T-lineage acute leukaemia. Although ETP-ALL frequently expresses stem-cell and myeloid-associated antigens (CD34, CD117, CD13, CD33), absence of MPO argues against myeloid lineage. AML may aberrantly express CD7 or other T-associated antigens but does not show lineage-defining cytoplasmic CD3.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: CD117 is a stem-cell marker shared by AML and ETP-ALL.
why_other_options_are_wrong.B: CD13 may be aberrantly expressed in ETP-ALL and AML.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: CD34 is a non-specific immaturity marker.
why_other_options_are_wrong.E: Blast percentage does not determine lineage.

frcpath_pearl: Do not diagnose AML because myeloid-associated antigens are present. Lineage-defining markers override aberrant antigen expression.
take_home_message: Cytoplasmic CD3 defines T-lineage; MPO defines myeloid lineage. ETP-ALL commonly co-expresses stem-cell and myeloid-associated antigens.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
